diagram_title: Webapp Registry Gateway — C4 Component Diagram

systems.name: OpenCode Platform
systems.description: AI coding agent platform with multi-interface access

systems.name: Webapp Backend
systems.description: Developer-deployed webapp (e.g. cecelearn) running on localhost

containers.name: C Gateway
containers.description: Root-level epoll-based reverse proxy and auth gateway

containers.name: webctl CLI
containers.description: Shell-based management tool for opencode runtime

components.name: HTTP Accumulator
components.description: Accumulates TCP segments until \r\n\r\n, parses method/path/cookie

components.name: Web Route Matcher
components.description: Prefix-matches request path against loaded route table

components.name: Route Table Loader
components.description: Parses /etc/opencode/web_routes.conf, sorts by prefix length desc

components.name: JWT Validator
components.description: Validates oc_jwt cookie, resolves uid via getpwnam

components.name: SIGHUP Handler
components.description: Sets reload flag on SIGHUP, checked in epoll loop

components.name: publish-route
components.description: Validates and appends route to web_routes.conf, sends SIGHUP

components.name: reload-routes
components.description: Sends SIGHUP to gateway PID to trigger route reload

components.name: Control Socket Server
components.description: Listens on /run/opencode-gateway/ctl.sock, accepts JSON commands for route management

components.name: Error Redirector
components.description: Returns 302 redirect to / when public route backend is unreachable

relationships.description: Passes parsed request path to route matcher
On match: initiates TCP connect + splice proxy to backend
On no match: falls through to JWT validation
On valid JWT: splice proxy to per-user daemon
Proxies public HTTP traffic to webapp frontend
Proxies public API traffic to webapp backend
Proxies authenticated traffic to per-user daemon
SIGHUP flag triggers route table reload
CLI connects to ctl.sock to send publish/remove/list commands
Updates in-memory route table on publish/remove
Flushes route table to disk after mutation
On match + connect fail: returns 302 redirect
Daemon registers webapp routes via ctl.sock
